chunks.text: THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, LARKANA Criminal Transfer Application No.S-32 of 2025 Applicant: Ghulam Murtaza Shah son of Ali Muzafar Shah Through Mr. Ahsan Ahmed Qureshi, Advocate. Respondents: Through Mr. Habibullah G. Ghouri, Advocate. The State: Through Mr. Sardar Ali, Deputy Prosecutor General, Sindh. Date of hearing: 23.07.2025 Date of Order: 23.07.2025 O R D E R KHALID HUSSAIN SHAHANI, J.- Through this Criminal Transfer Application, the applicant/complainant seeks transfer of Sessions Case No.484/2002, emanating from F.I.R. No.08/2000, registered with Police Station Nasirabad from the board of learned Additional Sessions Judge- II, Larkana to another Court having jurisdiction, mainly on the pretext that court is lying vacant. 2. Learned counsel for the applicant submits that the case was proceeded by the trial Court and was fixed for pronouncement of Judgment; however, due to transfer of the Presiding Officer, it could not be announced. Now the Court of learned Additional Sessions Judge-II, Larkana is lying vacant since February, 2025 and the parties are suffering, therefore, prays that the case may be transferred to another Court having jurisdiction. Learned counsel for the respondents as well as Deputy Prosecutor General, Sindh have candidly raised no objection for transfer of the case in the interest of justice. 2 3. Accordingly, by consent the Sessions Case No.484/2002, emanating from F.I.R. No.08/2000, registered with Police Station Nasirabad, under section 302, 324, P.P.C is withdrawn from the board of Additional Sessions Judge-II, Larkana and made over to the learned Sessions Judge, Larkana with direction to either proceed with the matter or transfer it to any other Court having jurisdiction for its expeditious disposal in accordance with law.

Accordingly, by consent the Sessions Case No.484/2002, emanating from F.I.R. No.08/2000, registered with Police Station Nasirabad, under section 302, 324, P.P.C is withdrawn from the board of Additional Sessions Judge-II, Larkana and made over to the learned Sessions Judge, Larkana with direction to either proceed with the matter or transfer it to any other Court having jurisdiction for its expeditious disposal in accordance with law. Instant Criminal Transfer Application stands disposed of in the above terms. J U D G E Manzoor
